reason: Alp Services / Ariaf Studies and Research influence-and-surveillance cluster. Leaked 'Abu Dhabi Secrets' files show Geneva PI firm Alp Services (Mario Brero) was contracted via the Emirati entity Ariaf for a multi-country smear/surveillance operation, supervised by an Emirati intelligence officer ('Sheikh Matar') and a ministerial-level aide to Tahnoun bin Zayed Al Nahyan, with bills routed to the UAE president. This is a UAE state-security-directed private-intelligence/influence op, distinct from DarkMatter SIGINT/offensive clusters and from Beacon Red. No clean formal-agency attribution beyond the state-security apparatus, so placed at the general UAE State Security service rather than a fabricated unit.

ToTok mass-surveillance program. NYT (2019) reported ToTok was a UAE mass-surveillance tool developed by Breej Holding, a front company of DarkMatter, and linked to Pax AI (co-located with the UAE Signals Intelligence Agency). This is a distinct DarkMatter program (consumer-app mass collection) separate from the existing DarkMatter clusters Project Raven (offensive hacking) and Karma (mobile zero-click), so it is placed at the DarkMatter Group service for consistency with sibling DarkMatter actors rather than merged into Project Raven.

UAE government as NSO Pegasus operator/customer. Citizen Lab's 'Million Dollar Dissident' report attributed the 2016 Pegasus targeting of UAE activist Ahmed Mansoor to the UAE government, and explicitly distinguishes this NSO-Pegasus operator from the DarkMatter-run Stealth Falcon cluster ('operated by different entities'). Deliberately NOT merged into ae/darkmatter/stealth-falcon (different sponsor-vendor relationship and toolset: FinFisher/Deadglyph vs commercial Pegasus). Placed at the general UAE State Security service as the government customer/operator; not attributing Pegasus to a specific named unit that reporting does not support.

ArcaneDoor / UAT4356, tracked by Microsoft as Storm-1849. Cisco Talos/CISA declined formal attribution in 2024, but Censys infrastructure analysis pointed to a China-based actor and Unit 42 subsequently attributed the Cisco ASA edge-device campaign to Storm-1849, 'a China-based threat group'; Recorded Future likewise reports Chinese actors. PRC state-nexus is therefore assessed (moderate confidence) and no longer genuinely disputed, but no specific agency is named, so placed at the PRC general service cn/mss per AUSPEX convention (nexus clear, unit unknown) rather than a fabricated unit.

Proofpoint 'Phish and Chips' cluster of three China-aligned actors (UNK_FistBump, UNK_DropPitch, UNK_SparkyCarp) that ramped up espionage targeting of Taiwan's semiconductor industry (design, manufacturing, supply chain, investment analysts) in 2025. Proofpoint assesses China-aligned, consistent with PRC's semiconductor self-sufficiency priorities. These are explicitly unattributed (UNK_) clusters, so PRC-nexus is clear but the exact unit is genuinely unknown -> placed at cn/mss per AUSPEX convention. Kept as a single combined espionage cluster matching the source's grouping; not merged into any existing roster actor.

Unit 42 CL-STA-0048 espionage operation against high-value South Asian targets (incl. a telecom), attributed to a China nexus with moderate-to-high confidence based on tooling, TTPs, infrastructure and victimology (UTC+8 working hours). Unit 42 notes only similarities to DragonRank and deliberately keeps CL-STA-0048 as a DISTINCT tracked cluster; Trend Micro separately links it to REF0657/Earth Lamia but the picture is unresolved. Per conservative-merge rule, treated as a distinct actor and placed at cn/mss (PRC-nexus clear, specific unit unknown) rather than merged.

Evasive Panda (aka Daggerfly, BRONZE HIGHLAND, StormBamboo, MITRE G1034), a China-aligned cyberespionage group active since at least 2012 using the MgBot/Nightdoor frameworks against Tibetan diaspora, Taiwan, Hong Kong and PRC pro-democracy targets, plus ISP supply-chain/DNS-hijacking ops. Classic PRC intelligence targeting; no specific named agency in public reporting, so placed at cn/mss per AUSPEX convention (nexus clear, unit unknown), consistent with how comparable China-aligned actors (Mustang Panda, BlackTech, etc.) are filed. Aliases not in existing roster, so a new PLACE not a merge.

FishMonger (aka Aquatic Panda, Earth Lusca-adjacent, TAG-22, Red Dev 10), the APT cluster operated by Chinese contractor i-Soon (Anxun). ESET's Operation FishMedley attributes the cluster to FishMonger/i-Soon with high confidence; the March 2025 US DOJ indictment of i-Soon staff states i-Soon conducted intrusions 'at the direction of the PRC's MPS and MSS.' Although loosely associated with the APT41/Winnti umbrella, FishMonger is tracked as its own cluster and is distinct from the existing i-Soon-adjacent roster actor Charcoal Typhoon, so not merged into APT41 or Charcoal Typhoon. Placed at cn/mss per AUSPEX convention (PRC-nexus clear; i-Soon directed by MSS/MPS), matching the sibling i-Soon cluster's cn/mss placement.

GENESIS PANDA, a CrowdStrike-tracked China-nexus, state-sponsored actor active since at least early 2024 across finance, telecom, tech and media in 11+ countries, cloud-conscious and assessed to act as an initial access broker for follow-on intelligence collection (reportedly overlapping with Trend Micro's Earth Lamia, though that alias is single-source and not relied on for placement). PRC-nexus clear, specific agency unknown -> placed at cn/mss per AUSPEX convention. Kept distinct from CL-STA-0048 in line with both vendors' separate tracking.

GLACIAL PANDA, a CrowdStrike-tracked China-nexus, state-sponsored adversary active since at least 2018 specializing in telecommunications espionage (call detail records / comms telemetry) against telco Linux systems, with targets concentrated in the South China Sea region and breaches in North/Central America; uses trojanized OpenSSH (ShieldSlide). Classic PRC SIGINT-support intelligence collection; no specific named agency in reporting, so placed at cn/mss per AUSPEX convention (nexus clear, unit unknown).

Houken, the ANSSI-named threat actor that exploited Ivanti CSA zero-days (CVE-2024-8190/-8963/-9380) against French government, telecom, media, finance and transport entities in late 2024. ANSSI links Houken to the previously documented UNC5174 (Mandiant), an MSS-affiliated initial access broker/contractor that gains access and sells it to a state-linked actor seeking intelligence. MSS nexus is explicitly assessed by ANSSI and Mandiant -> placed at cn/mss. UNC5174/Houken is not present in the existing roster, so a new PLACE rather than a merge.

SecurityScorecard STRIKE assesses LapDogs as a China-nexus covert ORB (operational relay box) network of 1,000+ compromised SOHO devices used for espionage staging, active since at least Sept 2023, targeting the US and East/Southeast Asia. PRC state-nexus is clear (Mandarin coder notes, victimology); the operating unit is not publicly named (STRIKE notes it was used by UAT-5918 'at least once', a weak single-use link, and UAT-5918 is not a rostered actor). Distinct from all rostered actors. Placed at PRC general service cn/mss per AUSPEX convention since no specific PLA unit is supported by reporting.

CrowdStrike Intelligence assesses LIMINAL PANDA as a China-nexus adversary targeting telecommunications providers in South Asia and Africa since at least 2020 (some activity previously tracked under LightBasin). State-nexus is PRC; no specific intelligence unit is named in public reporting. Distinct named adversary, not equivalent to any rostered actor. Placed at cn/mss per AUSPEX general-PRC convention.

Lotus Blossom (MITRE ATT&CK G0030; aka Spring Dragon, Billbug, Thrip, DRAGONFISH, Lotus Panda) is a long-running Chinese state-sponsored espionage group active since ~2009-2012, using the Sagerunex backdoor against government, telecom, media and manufacturing targets in SE Asia (Philippines, Vietnam, Hong Kong, Taiwan). PRC state-sponsored nexus is well-established; no specific PLA unit is publicly attributed. Distinct from rostered actors. Placed at cn/mss per AUSPEX convention. NOTE: Raspberry Typhoon (also in this batch) is the same group per G0030 and is merged into this id.

MITRE ATT&CK G0030 authoritatively lists Raspberry Typhoon (Microsoft, formerly RADIUM) as an associated-group name for Lotus Blossom, alongside Spring Dragon, Billbug, Thrip and DRAGONFISH. Microsoft's Raspberry Typhoon (China-based, targets South China Sea littoral states) corresponds to the same cluster. Well-established alias equivalence, so merged into the Lotus Blossom actor (placed in this same batch at cn/mss). RADIUM is the legacy Microsoft element name for the same actor.

Recorded Future Insikt Group attributes RedNovember (= the activity previously tracked as TAG-100; overlaps Microsoft Storm-2077) to a Chinese state-sponsored threat actor targeting government, defense and technology orgs worldwide via edge-device exploitation, Pantegana and Cobalt Strike (June 2024-July 2025). PRC state-sponsored nexus is clear; no specific unit named. TAG-100/Storm-2077 are not rostered actors, so distinct. Placed at cn/mss per AUSPEX convention.

Palo Alto Unit 42 (the sole discovering vendor) deliberately declines country-level attribution: they assess with high confidence only that TGR-STA-1030 / UNC6619 is 'a state-aligned group that operates out of Asia', and explicitly use the TGR-STA prefix as a placeholder while refining attribution. China-leaning indicators exist (Diaoyu Loader naming, GMT+8 activity, a 'JackMa' handle) but Unit 42 itself frames these as speculative, not confirmatory, and names no country or service. Asserting a PRC service (cn/mss) would over-claim beyond what the primary source supports, so the state-nexus is genuinely unresolved -> keep under unscoped (China-suspected). Reassess if a vendor firmly attributes it to the PRC.

Cisco Talos tracks UAT-7290 as a distinct China-nexus espionage cluster (active since 2022) targeting high-value telecom and critical infrastructure in South Asia. Talos explicitly does NOT directly attribute it to a PLA unit: it states UAT-7290 'shares a significant amount of overlap' in victimology/infrastructure/tooling with Red Foxtrot (which Recorded Future, separately, linked to PLA Unit 69010 in 2021), and also overlaps APT10. Per AUSPEX rules, a two-hop vendor-bridged overlap does not justify fabricating a specific PLA unit, so placed at the PRC general service cn/mss. Red Foxtrot / PLA Unit 69010 overlap noted but not asserted as the operating unit.

Cisco Talos assesses with high confidence that UAT-8302 is a China-nexus APT (active since late 2024) tasked with long-term access to government entities, targeting South America and SE Europe. It reuses tooling (NetDraft/FinalDraft, CloudSorcerer, VShell/SNOWLIGHT) shared across several China-nexus clusters but is tracked as a distinct group; none of those overlapping clusters are rostered actors. No specific intelligence unit is named. Placed at cn/mss per AUSPEX general-PRC convention.

Google Threat Intelligence Group / Mandiant assess UNC2814 (GRIDTIDE campaign) as a suspected PRC-nexus cyber-espionage group tracked since 2017, with confirmed intrusions in 42 countries against governments and telecoms, using the GRIDTIDE backdoor (Google Sheets API for C2). Suspected-PRC state-nexus is the discoverer's assessment; it remains an uncategorized (UNC) cluster with no named unit. Distinct from rostered actors. Placed at cn/mss per AUSPEX convention.

Mandiant tracks UNC3886 (MITRE G1048) as a China-nexus cyber-espionage group active since at least 2022, exploiting Fortinet/VMware/Juniper zero-days for stealthy long-term access against defense, technology and telecom targets (incl. Singapore's telecom sector). PRC state-nexus is the discoverer's assessment; no specific PLA/MSS unit is publicly named. Distinct uncategorized cluster, not equivalent to any rostered actor. Placed at cn/mss per AUSPEX general-PRC convention.

CrowdStrike tracks WARP PANDA as a China-nexus, cloud-conscious intrusion adversary (active since at least 2022) targeting legal/tech/manufacturing in North America and APAC via edge devices and vCenter, using BRICKSTORM/GuestConduit/Junction in support of PRC strategic objectives. Reporting indicates WARP PANDA corresponds to Mandiant's UNC5221, but that correspondence is 'same or closely related' (not a hard-confirmed single cluster) and UNC5221 is not a rostered actor, so no merge target exists -> placed as a distinct PRC-nexus actor. No specific unit named. Placed at cn/mss per AUSPEX convention.

Financially-motivated eCrime data-theft/extortion crew active since at least Oct 2025; CrowdStrike places it within 'The Com' English-speaking eCrime ecosystem, aligned with Scattered Spider/SLSH/ShinyHunters. No state nexus.

Commercial spyware vendor/consortium (Predator), not a state intelligence service. Sells to many governments (clients across Egypt, Saudi Arabia, Kazakhstan, etc.) and was OFAC-sanctioned as a private commercial entity; no single state operator attributed. Per AUSPEX convention commercial-spyware vendors are not a state service, so it stays in the criminal/ vendor bucket with null service (parallel to how the vendor itself, vs a specific government operator, is treated).

Financially-motivated ransomware operation (first seen Sep 2024) running opportunistic double-extortion via ClickFix; subject of a CISA/FBI #StopRansomware advisory describing it as financially motivated. No state nexus.

Financially-motivated cybercrime collective/alliance (Scattered Spider + LAPSUS$ + ShinyHunters, 'The Com'), extortion-as-a-service. Distinct named cluster tracked separately by CrowdStrike/Krebs; NOT merged into any single component (it is an alliance of all three, merging would misrepresent it). No state nexus.

Financially-motivated actor (active since ~2021) operating as a RaaS affiliate (Sabbath, Hive, BlackCat, LockBit, Embargo) now doing cloud-based ransomware/extortion via Entra ID. Microsoft explicitly calls it financially motivated; no state nexus.

Pro-Russian disinformation/influence operation. State alignment (Russia) is broadly assessed, but the responsible organ is genuinely disputed: France's authoritative VIGINUM/SGDSN technical report explicitly states it cannot confirm involvement of GRU officer Khoroshenky or GRU Unit 29155; Microsoft attributes direction to the Center for Geopolitical Expertise think tank; only single-source Ukrainian intelligence claims GRU Unit 29155 financing. No specific Russian service is confirmed, so placement under any GRU/FSB/SVR service would assert an attribution the primary sources decline to make. (Note: current placeholder namespace fr/unscoped is geographically misleading — the actor is Russian, surfaced via a French source; left unscoped rather than fabricating a Russian unit.)

Duqu 2.0 (2015 Kaspersky/P5+1-hotels intrusion) is widely attributed to Israel and specifically to IDF Unit 8200; it is a Duqu/Stuxnet-lineage toolset from the joint US-Israel program, and Unit 8200 is Israel's SIGINT/offensive-cyber corps. Placing under the existing il/idf-8200 service.

Distinct, well-documented domestic law-enforcement SIGINT cyber unit (Calcalist/Haaretz, 2022) that operated NSO Pegasus against Israeli citizens without warrants. It is an Israel Police unit (staffed by Unit 8200 alumni but organizationally separate from any listed intelligence service); no matching service exists, so defining a minimal Israel Police SIGINT service. Reporting gives no official internal unit number, so the service is named only as far as sourcing supports (Israel Police SIGINT cyber unit) rather than inventing a designation.

Anchoring event is the May 2020 Shahid Rajaee port disruption, attributed by US/foreign officials (Washington Post) to Israeli operatives; the head of Israeli military intelligence subsequently awarded certificates of appreciation to Unit 8200 troops for the covert operation (Times of Israel). Unit 8200 is the most specific service public reporting supports for this operator, so placing under existing il/idf-8200.

Bitter / TA397: Proofpoint+Threatray assess it 'highly likely' a state-backed actor collecting intelligence in the interests of the Indian state (IST working hours, India-aligned targeting of Pakistan/China interests); The Record reports new evidence linking it to the Indian government. No specific unit is publicly confirmed (no sourced NTRO link found), so per AUSPEX convention this civilian-style cyberespionage cluster is placed under India's general technical-intelligence service in/ntro — consistent with the existing in/ntro placement of the other India-attributed espionage actors (DoNot/Patchwork/SideWinder) and matching NTRO's intelligence-collection remit better than the military in/dcya. Not asserting a new specific unit.

Confucius: assessed since 2013 as a pro-India, state-sponsored APT targeting Pakistani military/nuclear personnel and Kashmir election officials (Lookout, Trend Micro, SecurityWeek). India attribution is well-supported; no specific unit is publicly confirmed, so placed under India's general technical-intelligence service in/ntro, consistent with AUSPEX's in/ntro home for India-attributed espionage clusters and NTRO's collection remit. Not asserting a new specific unit.

APT-K-47 / Mysterious Elephant (Knownsec designation, active since ~2022) targets Pakistan/Bangladesh/Turkey and shares tooling/targeting overlaps with India-aligned clusters (SideWinder, Patchwork, Confucius, Bitter). However, public reporting (Knownsec, Kaspersky, Recorded Future) explicitly assesses attribution as low confidence and does NOT directly attribute it to India or any specific Indian service. State nexus is genuinely unresolved, so it stays unscoped rather than being forced under in/ntro.

Agrius (aka Agonizing Serpens, Pink Sandstorm) is an Iranian wiper/fake-ransomware actor active since 2020 targeting Israel (MultiLayer/PartialWasher/BFG Agonizer wipers, Apostle, DEADWOOD). Check Point's Iran cyber-capability mapping links Agrius to MOIS. Distinct cluster: its 'BlackShadow' alias is a naming collision and does NOT equal AUSPEX's ir/irgc/black-shadow (DEV-0270 / Nemesis Kitten), so placed as its own MOIS actor rather than merged. State unit is named in reporting (MOIS) so it goes to ir/mois, not unscoped.

ESET assesses with medium confidence that BladedFeline (active since at least 2017, targeting Kurdish/Iraqi officials with Whisper backdoor, Laret/Pinar tunnels, PrimeCache IIS module) is a subgroup of OilRig/APT34. APT34 is consensus-attributed to Iran's MOIS (AUSPEX: ir/mois/apt34). Conservative choice: PLACE under ir/mois as its own named cluster rather than MERGE into apt34, since ESET tracks it as a distinct (sub)group and confidence is only medium.

Imperial Kitten (aka Tortoiseshell, Crimson Sandstorm, TA456, Yellow Liderc), active since at least 2017, targets Israeli/Middle East maritime, defense and tech via recruitment-themed social engineering. CrowdStrike assesses it 'likely fulfills Iranian strategic intelligence requirements associated with IRGC operations.' Consistent with AUSPEX filing other suspected-IRGC Kitten actors (APT33, APT35) under ir/irgc.

Scarred Manticore (Check Point/Sygnia) is an Iranian espionage actor using the LIONTAIL framework (HTTP.sys-abusing implants) against Middle East government/telecom. Its tooling is tied to the MOIS-sponsored DEV-0861 destructive attack on Albania. Check Point states it is 'likely related' to OilRig but that they 'do not have sufficient data to properly attribute' it to OilRig — so placed as its own MOIS-linked cluster, NOT merged into apt34.

Void Manticore is the same MOIS-affiliated cluster as AUSPEX's ir/mois/handala. That existing actor already carries aliases 'Storm-0842', 'Banished Kitten' and 'Dune' — and Void Manticore is tracked precisely as Storm-0842 (Microsoft) / Banished Kitten (CrowdStrike), with 'Handala Hack' as its current primary public persona (alongside earlier HomeLand Justice and Karma personas). Per Check Point and MITRE G1055 these are one actor. Direct alias match makes this a confident, conservative merge (also adding 'Red Sandstorm').

CrowdStrike and MITRE G1049 confirm GOLDEN CHOLLIMA / Citrine Sleet / Gleaming Pisces / AppleJeus / UNC4736 / UNC1720 are the same DPRK cryptocurrency-theft actor (a spin-off of Labyrinth Chollima). AUSPEX's kp/rgb/citrine-sleet already carries aliases 'AppleJeus', 'Labyrinth Chollima' and 'UNC4736' — direct overlap. Confident merge; also adding 'Gleaming Pisces'.

Ruby Sleet is Microsoft's current name for the activity Mandiant historically tracked as CERIUM and now folds into APT43 (= Kimsuky). AUSPEX's kp/rgb/kimsuky already carries the alias 'APT43'. Ruby Sleet / CERIUM is consistently described as Kimsuky activity. Merge inherits Kimsuky's RGB placement (the consensus attribution); adding aliases 'Ruby Sleet' and 'CERIUM'.

Mandiant/Google's UNC1069 is the same cluster Microsoft tracks as Sapphire Sleet, an offshoot of BlueNoroff focused on cryptocurrency theft (incl. the Axios npm supply-chain compromise). AUSPEX's kp/rgb/bluenoroff already carries the alias 'Sapphire Sleet' (and APT38 / Stardust Chollima / TA444). Confident merge into the BlueNoroff cluster.

DarkHotel (aka DUBNIUM, Tapaoux, Karba, APT-C-06) is frequently described as 'South Korea-linked', and Tencent has alleged ties to the ROK National Intelligence Service. However this attribution is not consensus: Kaspersky and others explicitly caution that DarkHotel's use of hotel infrastructure provides plausible deniability and that origin is an imperfect estimate. With the state nexus genuinely disputed and no firm consensus on a ROK service, it stays unscoped rather than being placed under kr/nis.

new_service.name: National Security Office (Office of the President)

reason: The ROK National Security Office (안보실) within the Office of the President is the real, publicly documented body that issued the February 2024 National Cybersecurity Strategy and the follow-on National Cybersecurity Basic Plan (the 'offensive cyber defense' posture, 100 tasks across 14 ministries). It is a national policy/strategy and oversight cluster, not an offensive intrusion unit — analogous to AUSPEX's existing policy/oversight services such as kr/mnd-cyber. No matching service exists, so a new source-backed service kr/nso is defined (not a fabricated intelligence unit). State nexus is clear (ROK), so it is placed rather than left unscoped.

APT-C-23 and Arid Viper are the same intrusion set tracked under different names. Malpedia (Fraunhofer FKIE) lists APT-C-23 as a primary synonym of Arid Viper (alongside Bearded Barbie, Desert Falcon, Two-tailed Scorpion); Kaspersky/Cybereason both place them as the single 'Gaza Cybergang Group 2 (Arid Viper / Desert Falcons / APT-C-23)' sub-cluster. Confident, well-sourced equivalence. Merging APT-C-23 into the more commonly-used canonical Arid Viper and adding APT-C-23 as an alias.

Arid Viper / APT-C-23 (Gaza Cybergang Group 2) is only ever attributed as 'suspected Hamas-aligned' — public reporting (SentinelOne, Kaspersky, Proofpoint) names NO specific Hamas cyberwarfare unit or Palestinian intelligence/government organization. The 'Hamas cyberwarfare division' framing (Cybereason) is a loose affiliation, not a concretely named org. No ps/ service exists, and defining one would fabricate a specific intelligence unit beyond what reporting supports. State/Hamas nexus is suspected/disputed and no specific org is attributable -> keep unscoped.

Molerats (TA402 / Gaza Cybergang Group 1) is consistently tracked as a DISTINCT sub-group from Arid Viper (Group 2) by Kaspersky, Proofpoint, Cybereason and MITRE (G0021) — they share victims/TTPs and show post-2018 consolidation overlaps, but vendors keep them separate, so a merge would be too aggressive. Like Arid Viper it is only 'suspected Hamas-aligned' with no specific named Hamas unit or Palestinian intel org in public reporting, and no ps/ service exists. Nexus suspected/disputed, no specific org -> keep unscoped, kept distinct.

Google GTIG attributes UNC4221 (CERT-UA: UAC-0185) only as 'Russia-linked' and explicitly does NOT tie it to a specific intelligence service (GRU/FSB/SVR); GTIG keeps it separate from APT44/Sandworm (which they attribute to GRU GTsST). It is an espionage cluster, so ru/proxies (cybercrime/hacktivist-proxy bucket) is inappropriate, and any specific FSB/GRU/SVR service would overstate attribution. Russia-nexus but the unit is genuinely unknown -> keep unscoped.

Volexity assesses only MEDIUM confidence that UTA0355 is Russian and ties it to no specific intelligence service; they explicitly track it as a separate entity (continuation of the April-2025 cluster) and do not equate it with APT29 despite shared device-code/OAuth phishing TTPs. Because even the state attribution is medium-confidence (not 'state-clear'), it does not qualify for the Russian general service; unit and firm state attribution both unresolved -> keep unscoped.

Citizen Lab attributes the KINGDOM Pegasus operator to a Saudi Arabia-linked government customer of NSO Group (targeting Ben Hubbard, Omar Abdulaziz, Ghanem al-Masarir, Yahya Assiri, an Amnesty staffer) but names NO specific Saudi organizational unit — it is an NSO customer-operator cluster, not an attributed Saudi service. Per the batch rule for state-sponsored commercial-spyware operators, keep unscoped unless a specific operator org is firmly attributed; no specific Saudi org is named and no sa/ service exists -> keep unscoped (do not fabricate a Saudi unit).

Placeholder 'us/unscoped' is wrong: DanaBot operators (CrowdStrike: SCULLY SPIDER) are a Russia-based eCrime MaaS crew — the DOJ Operation Endgame indictment names Aleksandr Stepanov and Artem Kalinkin of Novosibirsk, Russia. Beyond ordinary cybercrime, a Russian-state ESPIONAGE nexus is firmly attributed: DanaBot sub-botnets 24 and 25 targeted diplomatic/government/military bodies in North America and Europe for intelligence-gathering on behalf of Russian government interests, with documented Kremlin tolerance and use supporting the invasion of Ukraine. This matches the AUSPEX ru/proxies pattern for Russian criminal ecosystems with an established state nexus (Conti, LockBit, Cl0p, Akira). Distinct from existing roster (no DanaBot/Scully Spider present). Canonical actor slug ru/proxies/danabot.
